name: Aluminum Nitride Laser Cleaning Dataset
description: Comprehensive laser cleaning parameters and material properties for Aluminum Nitride. Includes thermal, optical, mechanical, and laser interaction properties validated against industry standards.

license.name: Creative Commons Attribution 4.0 International
license.description: Free to share and adapt with attribution

measurementTechnique: Laser ablation testing, material characterization, spectroscopy

includedInDataCatalog.name: Z-Beam Material Properties Database
includedInDataCatalog.description: Comprehensive laser cleaning parameters and material properties for industrial applications

powerRange.description: Optimal average power for ceramic cleaning

spotSize.description: Beam spot diameter for effective cleaning resolution

repetitionRate.description: Pulse frequency for controlled material removal

energyDensity.description: Energy per unit area for surface cleaning

pulseWidth.description: Pulse duration for minimal thermal penetration

scanSpeed.description: Beam travel speed for uniform coverage

passCount.description: Number of cleaning cycles for complete removal

overlapRatio.description: Beam overlap for consistent surface treatment

power.description: Typical range for industrial cleaning of ceramics like AlN to avoid thermal damage; starting at 100 W balances efficiency and safety. Max ~200 W before risking substrate ablation. Source: IPG Photonics laser cleaning systems specs for delicate materials.

scan_speed.description: Effective speeds for contaminant removal on AlN; slower for heavy contamination (500-1000 mm/s), faster for light (2000-5000 mm/s). Recommended 2000 mm/s for optimal balance. Source: Industry standards from Laserax equipment manuals.

pulse_width.description: Nanosecond regime preferred for AlN cleaning to minimize heat-affected zone (HAZ) due to high thermal conductivity; picosecond (ps) or femtosecond for ultra-precision but less common industrially. 20 ns recommended for low HAZ. Source: Laser physics studies in Optics & Laser Technology (e.g., ns pulses for ceramics).

repetition_rate.description: Typical for 1064 nm cleaning; 50 kHz recommended for AlN to achieve fast cleaning without excessive heat accumulation, trading off speed vs. thermal load. Higher rates (>80 kHz) increase heat on reflective AlN. Source: Academic literature in Applied Surface Science on pulse repetition effects.

energy_density.description: Fluence range for safe contaminant ablation on AlN; damage threshold ~2-3 J/cm² at 1064 nm due to AlN's high melting point and reflectivity. 1.5 J/cm² recommended for cleaning without substrate harm. Source: Ablation threshold studies in Journal of Applied Physics for nitrides.

spot_size.description: Beam diameter for uniform cleaning; smaller (50-100 μm) for fine details, larger (200-500 μm) for area prep on AlN. 200 μm recommended for industrial surface preparation. Source: Manufacturer specs from Coherent and Trumpf laser systems.

pass_count.description: Single pass for light contamination, multi-pass (2-5) for heavy; 2 passes recommended for complete AlN cleaning to ensure uniformity without overexposure. Source: Industry case studies in laser cleaning of ceramics (e.g., SPIE proceedings).

overlap_ratio.description: Ensures uniform coverage; 60% recommended for AlN to avoid gaps or overheating, typical range 30-80% based on contamination. Source: Process optimization in Journal of Laser Applications.
